expectations.text: Permission defined in restricted_reports.permissions.yml with 'title' key (permission string must match what route uses)
expectations.evidence: restricted_reports.permissions.yml defines 'view restricted reports' with title: 'View restricted reports'. The route uses _custom_access which internally checks this exact permission string via AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission($account, 'view restricted reports').

expectations.text: Route _permission requirement references the EXACT permission string defined in .permissions.yml
expectations.evidence: restricted_reports.routing.yml uses '_custom_access: \Drupal\restricted_reports\Access\RestrictedReportsAccessChecker::access' instead of '_permission: view restricted reports'. While the custom access checker internally references the correct permission string, the route itself does not use the _permission requirement key.

expectations.text: If custom access checking code exists, it returns AccessResult objects (AccessResult::allowed(), AccessResult::forbidden(), AccessResult::neutral()) -- NOT bare booleans (TRUE/FALSE)
expectations.evidence: src/Access/RestrictedReportsAccessChecker.php line 25: 'return AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission($account, 'view restricted reports');' -- returns AccessResult object, no bare booleans.

expectations.text: If AccessResult objects are used, they include ->addCacheContexts(['user.permissions']) or ->addCacheTags() for cache-aware access
expectations.evidence: RestrictedReportsAccessChecker.php line 25 uses AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission() which automatically adds 'user.permissions' cache context internally (documented Drupal core behavior). The docblock on line 22 confirms: 'Cache context is added automatically by allowedIfHasPermission().'.

expectations.text: Does NOT use hook_permission() (D7 pattern) -- permissions are in .permissions.yml
expectations.evidence: Searched all module files for 'hook_permission' -- no matches found. Permissions are defined in restricted_reports.permissions.yml.

expectations.text: Does NOT implement manual CSRF token checking -- would use _csrf_token route option if CSRF protection needed
expectations.evidence: Searched all module files for 'csrf' (case-insensitive) -- no matches found. No manual CSRF token checking implemented.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en restricted_reports returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: ddev drush en restricted_reports -y returned '[notice] Already installed: restricted_reports' with EXIT_CODE=0.

expectations.text: E2E: Anonymous gets 403 -- navigate to /reports/restricted as anonymous and verify access denied
E2E: Admin gets 200 -- navigate to /reports/restricted as admin and verify page loads
expectations.evidence: After authenticating via drush uli, curl -sk -b cookies -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://d10-access-with.ddev.site/reports/restricted returned 200.

expectations.text: Permission defined in restricted_reports.permissions.yml with 'title' key (permission string must match what route uses)
expectations.evidence: restricted_reports.permissions.yml defines permission 'view restricted reports' with title: 'View restricted reports'. The custom access method in RestrictedReportsController::access() checks this exact permission string via AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission($account, 'view restricted reports').

expectations.text: Route _permission requirement references the EXACT permission string defined in .permissions.yml
expectations.evidence: The route in restricted_reports.routing.yml uses '_custom_access: \Drupal\restricted_reports\Controller\RestrictedReportsController::access' (line 7), NOT '_permission'. While the custom access method does check the correct permission string, the route itself does not use the _permission requirement key. The expectation specifically requires a _permission route requirement.

expectations.text: If custom access checking code exists, it returns AccessResult objects (AccessResult::allowed(), AccessResult::forbidden(), AccessResult::neutral()) -- NOT bare booleans (TRUE/FALSE)
expectations.evidence: RestrictedReportsController.php line 36: 'return AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission($account, 'view restricted reports');' -- returns an AccessResult object, not a bare boolean.

expectations.text: If AccessResult objects are used, they include ->addCacheContexts(['user.permissions']) or ->addCacheTags() for cache-aware access
expectations.evidence: AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission() internally calls ->addCacheContexts(['user.permissions']) (verified in Drupal core AccessResult.php line 115). So although the module doesn't explicitly call addCacheContexts, the helper method it uses does, ensuring cache-aware access.

expectations.text: Does NOT use hook_permission() (D7 pattern) -- permissions are in .permissions.yml
expectations.evidence: Searched all module files for 'hook_permission' -- no matches found. Permissions are correctly defined in restricted_reports.permissions.yml.

expectations.text: Does NOT implement manual CSRF token checking -- would use _csrf_token route option if CSRF protection needed
expectations.evidence: Searched all module files for 'csrf' (case-insensitive) -- no matches found. No manual CSRF token checking is implemented.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en restricted_reports returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: Command 'ddev drush en restricted_reports -y' returned 'Already installed: restricted_reports' with exit code 0.

expectations.text: E2E: Anonymous gets 403 -- navigate to /reports/restricted as anonymous and verify access denied
E2E: Admin gets 200 -- navigate to /reports/restricted as admin and verify page loads
expectations.evidence: After logging in as admin (uid 1) via drush uli one-time login, curl with session cookie to https://d10-access-without.ddev.site/reports/restricted returned 200.
